concept: Weather / Atmospheric Conditions at a Place and Time
description: Atmospheric conditions at a stated place and time: today's sky, not the pattern it belongs to. One day of weather can offer a single data point toward a claim about climate, never the claim itself.
sound_symbolism: The complete wind-bearing opening 'howe', shared with 'howeli' (wind), brings changing air into motion. Final 'nu' closes a temporary episode at a stated place and time, making weather transient without making it trivial.
grammatical_notes: A mass noun: 'ha howenu sulae nai.' (This weather is warm.) Name place, time, source, and exact variables when they matter. Distinct from individual roots such as 'pheralu' (rain) and 'kurisha' (storm), from 'meluna' (season), and from 'meluri' (climate).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Weather language supports practical care, work, access, shelter, water, and energy decisions while keeping one event distinct from climate evidence.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Close attention to immediate sky, wind, rain, heat, and cold remains a situated practice rather than an abstract forecast alone.

tags.nature: atmospheric conditions and events at a place and time
tags.temporal: a short-duration environmental condition or event
